title: Long life: Doctors these days are so sexy!
gist: A personal essay by actor Sheila Hancock recounting an NHS hospital stay after cardiac arrest, praising the multinational, highly skilled staff who treated her — and closing on the tension that frontline NHS workers admired inside the ward face hostility and far-right intimidation (a nurse nervous of Tommy Robinson's presence) outside it.

insights.claim: NHS frontline care in a London hospital is delivered substantially by staff of diverse national origins, whose skill and dedication the author credits with saving multiple critically ill patients.
insights.speaker: Sheila Hancock
insights.so_what: First-hand testimony underscores NHS dependence on migrant labour — a sector exposed to UK immigration-policy tightening.
insights.quote: I watched professionals from diverse countries of origin, deploying their incredible skill and dedication to snatch us from the jaws of death.
insights.locator: I lay there immobile for three days

insights.claim: NHS staff face a gap between being respected for skill inside the ward and meeting 'mindless hatred' and far-right intimidation outside it, with one nurse describing nervousness about going home when activist Tommy Robinson visits.
insights.speaker: Sheila Hancock
insights.so_what: Signals rising hostility toward migrant key-workers as a social-risk backdrop to UK far-right politics.
insights.quote: Back in the real world, they are rewarded for their care with mindless hatred.
insights.locator: In the enclosed world of the hospital ward

insights.claim: A consumer smartwatch detected the author's irregular heartbeat (swinging between 40 and 160 bpm) and flagged her fall, illustrating wearable health tech's growing role in cardiac monitoring.
insights.speaker: Sheila Hancock
insights.so_what: Anecdotal datapoint for the consumer-health-wearables thesis as an aging-population monitoring tool.
insights.quote: according to my clever watch, pirouetted between 40 and 160 beats-per-minute
insights.locator: I have for some time battled
